articles.headline: Brussels Airlines BRU-JFK: distinctly Belgian and rather decadent

articles.takeaway: Brussels Airlines' fragmented business class cabin configurations across its aging A330 fleet create operational complexity that rivals often exploit, particularly on competitive transatlantic routes where product consistency drives premium bookings.

articles.headline: How Much Does An Airbus A350-900 Cost In 2026?
articles.blurb: Despite its high list price, the A350-900's presence on the global stage is massive.
articles.takeaway: Airlines typically negotiate 40-50% discounts off Airbus's $445M list price for A350-900s, making actual acquisition costs significantly lower than published figures suggest.

articles.headline: NTSB Team Still En Route To LaGuardia: Delayed By TSA Lines
articles.blurb: The NTSB is undertaking a complete walking inspection of the LaGuardia crash site, facing a tremendous amount of debris strewn across the runway and taxiways.
articles.takeaway: TSA security delays are impacting accident investigation timelines, highlighting potential friction between aviation safety and airport security protocols.

articles.headline: The Evolution of Aviation SMS
articles.blurb: From the Wright brothers to the 2024 FAA rule — how aviation safety evolved from reactive investigation to proactive risk management.
articles.takeaway: Modern SMS shifts aviation's risk approach from investigating accidents after they occur to identifying and mitigating hazards before incidents happen.

articles.headline: The B-2 Spirit Bomber’s Flat Architecture Explained: Radar Evasion Tactics For Pilots
articles.blurb: Inside America's tailless, flying wing stealth bomber.
articles.takeaway: The B-2's blended flying-wing design eliminates vertical surfaces that reflect radar signals, fundamentally reducing detectability compared to conventional fuselage-and-tail aircraft architectures.

articles.headline: Air Canada Flight Attendant Thrown 100 Meters In Jump Seat During LaGuardia Crash
articles.blurb: A flight attendant's miraculous survival after being thrown more than 100 meters from an Air Canada Express plane during a fatal crash at LGA.
articles.takeaway: Extreme g-forces during crash impacts can eject occupants despite restraint systems, highlighting critical gaps in jump seat safety engineering standards.

articles.headline: Selecting Risk Controls and Accepting Residual Risk
articles.blurb: Where Safety Risk Management moves from analysis to action — how organizations develop controls under §5.55(c)–(d).
articles.takeaway: Organizations must document why residual risks remain acceptable after implementing controls, creating a compliance and liability trail for regulators and accident investigators.

articles.headline: Why Newer Aircraft Aren’t Always Better
articles.blurb: The newest jets promise efficiency and luxury, but hidden trade-offs lurk beneath the shiny exterior.
articles.takeaway: Operators must weigh modern aircraft's fuel efficiency gains against higher maintenance complexity, parts obsolescence risks, and total cost of ownership that often exceed older platforms.

articles.blurb: The jet will be built on stealth, speed, and strategy.
articles.takeaway: Boeing's F-47 program represents a strategic pivot to recapture fighter jet market share through next-generation stealth capabilities rather than competing on traditional performance metrics.

articles.takeaway: Wire strikes remain a leading fatal helicopter hazard despite pilot overconfidence in reaction time, requiring enhanced situational awareness and detection technologies.

articles.headline: Extra Security: Why All US F-14 Tomcats Were Destroyed In The Arizona Desert
articles.blurb: How the US ensured that F-14 parts woudn't end up in enemy hands.
articles.takeaway: The systematic destruction of retired F-14s in Arizona reflected Cold War-era concerns that advanced fighter components could be reverse-engineered by adversaries if allowed to enter the global surplus market.

articles.headline: 110 Soldiers Onboard: Lockheed C-130 Crashes
articles.blurb: The Lockheed C-130 Hercules, a staple of military aviation, crashed in Colombia's Amazon region, leaving many questions about the cause.
articles.takeaway: The C-130's ubiquity across 70+ air forces masks critical operational risks in remote, high-altitude terrain where maintenance and emergency response capabilities are severely limited.

articles.headline: Towards Environmentally Sustainable Aviation: A Review on Operational Optimization
articles.blurb: Growing air traffic is straining airports and airspace while raising environmental concerns, and demand is only expected to increase. This paper reviews how advanced technologies and methods can expand capacity and improve efficiency across airline, airport, and flight operations. It also highlights emerging trends and gaps for future research in sustainable aviation.
articles.takeaway: Operational efficiency gains through technology integration offer near-term environmental benefits without waiting for fleet-wide aircraft replacement cycles.
keywords: aviation; optimization; operations research; sustainability; environmental impacts

articles.headline: Remembering the Green Ramp Disaster
articles.blurb: Tragedy struck on March 23, 1994, at Pope Air Force Base, North Carolina, on an aircraft parking ramp known as ‘Green Ramp.’ A number of paratroopers were lost and several injured after flames and debris rained down on their training and assembly area after a mid-air collision between a fighter j...
articles.takeaway: The 1994 Green Ramp disaster killed 24 paratroopers during training, establishing critical lessons in military airfield safety protocols and airspace management near ground troop assembly areas.

articles.takeaway: NASA's GUARDIAN system offers aviation and maritime industries a critical 10-15 minute advance warning window by detecting ionospheric disturbances before tsunami waves arrive.
